definition: The activity of managing money, especially in relation to businesses or governments.
example: The company hired a new manager to oversee its finance department.
exampleVi: Công ty đã thuê một quản lý mới để giám sát bộ phận tài chính.
collocations: corporate finance, personal finance, finance department, public finance

definition: Coins or paper notes that are used to buy things, pay for services, or settle debts.
example: It takes a lot of money to start a new business.
exampleVi: Cần rất nhiều tiền để khởi nghiệp một doanh nghiệp mới.
collocations: raise money, save money, spend money, sum of money

definition: Money or property that is used to start a business or produce more wealth.
example: The startup raised one million dollars in capital from investors.
exampleVi: Công ty khởi nghiệp đã huy động được một triệu đô la vốn từ các nhà đầu tư.
collocations: working capital, venture capital, raise capital, share capital

definition: An amount of money saved or made available for a particular purpose.
example: The company established a fund to support small businesses.
exampleVi: Công ty đã thành lập một quỹ để hỗ trợ các doanh nghiệp nhỏ.
collocations: pension fund, mutual fund, raise funds, trust fund

definition: The act of putting money or effort into something to make a profit or achieve a result.
example: Giving your children a good education is a wise long-term investment.
exampleVi: Cho con bạn một nền giáo dục tốt là một sự đầu tư dài hạn khôn ngoan.
collocations: make an investment, foreign investment, return on investment, long-term investment

definition: To put money, time, or effort into something to make a profit or get an advantage.
example: It is wise to invest a portion of your income in mutual funds.
exampleVi: Đầu tư một phần thu nhập của bạn vào các quỹ tương hỗ là điều khôn ngoan.
collocations: invest in stocks, invest heavily, invest wisely

definition: A share representing part ownership in a company, traded on a stock exchange.
example: The price of the company's stock rose sharply after the announcement.
exampleVi: Giá cổ phiếu của công ty đã tăng mạnh sau thông báo.
collocations: buy/sell stock, stock price, stock market, common stock

definition: One of the equal parts into which the ownership of a company is divided.
example: She bought 100 shares of the company's stock.
exampleVi: Cô ấy đã mua 100 cổ phần của công ty.
collocations: buy shares, ordinary shares, market share, share price

definition: An official paper given by a government or company to promise they will pay back borrowed money with interest.
example: The government issued bonds to raise money for infrastructure projects.
exampleVi: Chính phủ đã phát hành trái phiếu để huy động vốn cho các dự án cơ sở hạ tầng.
collocations: government bond, corporate bond, buy bonds, treasury bond

definition: Something of value owned by a person, company, or organization.
example: These shares are a valuable asset.
exampleVi: Những cổ phiếu này là tài sản có giá trị.
collocations: current asset, fixed asset, liquid asset, asset management

definition: The legal responsibility for something, or an amount of money that a company owes.
example: The company's liabilities exceeded its total assets.
exampleVi: Khoản nợ phải trả của công ty vượt quá tổng tài sản.
collocations: current liability, limited liability, liability insurance, tax liability

definition: A plan showing how much money a person or organization will earn and spend over a period.
example: The finance team prepared next year's budget for the department.
exampleVi: Đội ngũ tài chính đã chuẩn bị ngân sách năm sau cho bộ phận.
collocations: annual budget, cut the budget, within budget, over budget

definition: The money that you spend on something or that is needed for a particular activity.
example: Buying the car was a big expense, but will be worth it in the long run.
exampleVi: Mua một chiếc ô tô là một khoản chi phí lớn nhưng sẽ có giá trị về lâu dài.
collocations: travel expenses, operating expense, at the expense of, business expense

definition: The total amount of money received by a company or organization from its activities.
example: The company's revenue increased by ten percent last quarter.
exampleVi: Doanh thu của công ty đã tăng 10% trong quý trước.
collocations: generate revenue, annual revenue, tax revenue, advertising revenue

definition: Money that is earned from work, investments, or business activities.
example: His monthly income is not enough to cover all his expenses.
exampleVi: Thu nhập hàng tháng của anh ấy không đủ để trang trải mọi chi phí.
collocations: monthly income, disposable income, annual income, source of income

definition: Money paid to the government, based on your income, goods you buy, or company profits.
example: Every citizen is required to pay income tax each year.
exampleVi: Mọi công dân đều phải nộp thuế thu nhập hàng năm.
collocations: income tax, pay tax, tax return, tax evasion

definition: An official examination of an organization's accounts and financial records.
example: The accounting firm conducted an audit of the company's financial records.
exampleVi: Công ty kiểm toán đã tiến hành kiểm toán hồ sơ tài chính của công ty.
collocations: financial audit, conduct an audit, internal audit, tax audit

definition: A large amount of money, property, or valuable possessions that a person or country owns.
example: He built his wealth through smart investments over many years.
exampleVi: Anh ấy đã xây dựng sự giàu có của mình qua nhiều năm đầu tư khôn ngoan.
collocations: personal wealth, acquire wealth, distribution of wealth, wealth management

definition: The state of being extremely poor and lacking money to satisfy basic needs like food and shelter.
example: The new policy aims to reduce poverty in rural areas.
exampleVi: Chính sách mới nhằm giảm nghèo ở các vùng nông thôn.
collocations: extreme poverty, live in poverty, eradicate poverty, poverty line

definition: A general and continuous increase in prices, leading to a fall in the purchasing value of money.
example: High inflation reduced people's purchasing power this year.
exampleVi: Lạm phát cao đã làm giảm sức mua của người dân trong năm nay.
collocations: high inflation, rate of inflation, combat inflation, run-away inflation

definition: The buying, selling, or conversion of currencies or financial assets.
example: The stock exchange is a marketplace where securities are traded.
exampleVi: Sở giao dịch chứng khoán là thị trường nơi chứng khoán được giao dịch.
collocations: in exchange for, foreign exchange, exchange of ideas, stock exchange

definition: A measurement of the speed, number, or frequency of something in relation to another.
example: The bank raised its interest rate last month.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng đã tăng lãi suất vào tháng trước.
collocations: interest rate, exchange rate, unemployment rate, growth rate

definition: A part of a company's profits that is paid regularly to its shareholders.
example: The company paid a dividend of two dollars per share.
exampleVi: Công ty đã trả cổ tức 2 đô la trên mỗi cổ phần.
collocations: pay a dividend, dividend yield, dividend payout, stock dividend

definition: The total amount of profit or return produced by an investment, usually shown as a percentage.
example: This bond offers a yield of five percent per year.
exampleVi: Trái phiếu này mang lại lợi suất 5% mỗi năm.
collocations: high yield, bond yield, annual yield, yield to maturity

definition: The possibility of something bad happening, such as losing money on an investment.
example: Investing in the stock market always involves some risk.
exampleVi: Đầu tư vào thị trường chứng khoán luôn tiềm ẩn rủi ro.
collocations: take a risk, reduce risk, high risk, financial risk

definition: To spread investments across different assets or industries in order to reduce overall risk.
example: Investors are advised to diversify their portfolios.
exampleVi: Các nhà đầu tư được khuyên nên đa dạng hóa danh mục đầu tư của mình.
collocations: diversify a portfolio, diversify investments, diversify products

definition: A person or group that owns shares in a company.
example: Shareholders voted to approve the merger.
exampleVi: Các cổ đông đã bỏ phiếu thông qua việc sáp nhập.
collocations: major shareholder, shareholder meeting, shareholder value

definition: A person or organization that puts money into financial schemes or assets to earn a profit.
example: Foreign investors are showing more interest in the local market.
exampleVi: Các nhà đầu tư nước ngoài đang thể hiện sự quan tâm nhiều hơn đến thị trường trong nước.
collocations: foreign investor, retail investor, institutional investor, private investor

definition: The system of money that is used in a particular country.
example: The value of the local currency dropped against the dollar.
exampleVi: Giá trị của đồng tiền địa phương đã giảm so với đô la.
collocations: foreign currency, national currency, local currency, currency exchange

definition: A place or system where buyers and sellers buy and sell goods, services, or financial assets.
example: The stock market fell sharply on Monday.
exampleVi: Thị trường chứng khoán đã giảm mạnh vào thứ Hai.
collocations: stock market, market share, global market, target market

definition: An extra charge paid for borrowing money, or earned for saving money, usually shown as a percentage.
example: The loan has an annual interest rate of six percent.
exampleVi: Khoản vay này có lãi suất hàng năm là 6%.
collocations: interest rate, compound interest, pay interest, earn interest

definition: A person or group that has an interest or concern in a company's decisions or performance.
example: The company consulted all its stakeholders before making the decision.
exampleVi: Công ty đã tham khảo ý kiến của tất cả các bên liên quan trước khi đưa ra quyết định.
collocations: key stakeholder, stakeholder management, consult stakeholders

definition: The value of a company's shares, or the value of a property after all debts are paid.
example: The founders retained fifty percent equity in the company.
exampleVi: Những người sáng lập giữ lại 50% vốn chủ sở hữu trong công ty.
collocations: private equity, brand equity, home equity, raise equity

definition: A collection of financial investments, such as stocks, bonds, and cash, held by an investor.
example: She manages a diverse investment portfolio for her clients.
exampleVi: Cô ấy quản lý một danh mục đầu tư đa dạng cho khách hàng của mình.
collocations: investment portfolio, diversify a portfolio, manage a portfolio

definition: An estimate or calculation of a future situation based on current trends and data.
example: The company released its sales projections for next year.
exampleVi: Công ty đã công bố dự báo doanh số cho năm sau.
collocations: sales projection, financial projection, make projections, long-term projection

definition: A long and severe period of economic decline with high unemployment and low business activity.
example: The Great Depression led to widespread unemployment in the 1930s.
exampleVi: Cuộc Đại suy thoái đã dẫn đến tình trạng thất nghiệp lan rộng vào những năm 1930.
collocations: economic depression, Great Depression, trigger a depression

definition: A general and continuous decrease in the level of prices in an economy.
example: Deflation can discourage consumer spending as prices keep falling.
exampleVi: Giảm phát có thể khiến người tiêu dùng hạn chế chi tiêu vì giá cả liên tục giảm.
collocations: rate of deflation, economic deflation, combat deflation

definition: A substance or raw material that can be bought and sold, such as oil, gold, or grain.
example: Oil is one of the most actively traded commodities in the world.
exampleVi: Dầu mỏ là một trong những hàng hóa được giao dịch sôi động nhất thế giới.
collocations: commodity market, commodity prices, basic commodity, agricultural commodity

definition: A period of temporary economic decline during which trade and industrial activity are reduced.
example: Many businesses closed during the recession.
exampleVi: Nhiều doanh nghiệp đã đóng cửa trong thời kỳ suy thoái.
collocations: economic recession, global recession, deep recession, slip into recession

definition: A statement of what is expected to happen in the future based on information that is available now.
example: Analysts released their economic forecast for the coming year.
exampleVi: Các nhà phân tích đã công bố dự báo kinh tế cho năm tới.
collocations: economic forecast, financial forecast, weather forecast, sales forecast

definition: The value of one currency compared to another, showing how much one currency is worth in the other.
example: The exchange rate between the dollar and the euro changes daily.
exampleVi: Tỷ giá hối đoái giữa đô la và euro thay đổi hàng ngày.
collocations: exchange rate fluctuation, fixed exchange rate, floating exchange rate

definition: Money that is earned in trade or business after paying all the costs.
example: The company made a large profit this year.
exampleVi: Công ty đã thu được lợi nhuận lớn trong năm nay.
collocations: make a profit, net profit, operating profit, profit margin

definition: The state of being unable to pay your debts as they become due.
example: The company faces insolvency.
exampleVi: Công ty phải đối mặt với tình trạng phá sản.
collocations: corporate insolvency, face insolvency, insolvency proceedings

definition: The state of having enough money to pay all your debts.
example: Maintaining solvency is essential for a business to survive.
exampleVi: Duy trì khả năng thanh toán là điều cần thiết để doanh nghiệp tồn tại.
collocations: maintain solvency, financial solvency, solvency ratio

definition: The failure to pay back borrowed money or meet a financial agreement.
example: He failed to make payments on time and is now in default.
exampleVi: Anh ta đã không thanh toán đúng hạn và hiện đang bị vỡ nợ.
collocations: default on a loan, risk of default, in default of, default rate

definition: A legal state of a person or business that cannot pay their debts.
example: The company filed for bankruptcy after years of losses.
exampleVi: Công ty đã nộp đơn xin phá sản sau nhiều năm thua lỗ.
collocations: file for bankruptcy, go bankrupt, prevent bankruptcy, declare bankruptcy

definition: An amount of money a company or person loses when expenses are greater than revenue.
example: The company reported a significant financial loss in the third quarter.
exampleVi: Công ty đã báo cáo một khoản lỗ tài chính đáng kể trong quý ba.
collocations: make a loss, net loss, financial loss, cover the losses

definition: The use of borrowed money to buy assets and increase potential returns on investment.
example: The firm used leverage to expand its business without raising new equity.
exampleVi: Công ty đã sử dụng đòn bẩy tài chính để mở rộng kinh doanh mà không cần huy động thêm vốn.
collocations: financial leverage, high leverage, leverage ratio

definition: Money that is owed to someone else.
example: He worked hard to pay off his debt.
exampleVi: Anh ấy đã làm việc chăm chỉ để trả hết nợ.
collocations: run into debt, pay off debt, outstanding debt, bad debt

definition: Money that is owed and should have been paid earlier.
example: The tenant was three months in arrears with his rent.
exampleVi: Người thuê nhà đã nợ đọng tiền thuê ba tháng.
collocations: in arrears, rent arrears, salary arrears, arrears of interest

definition: A risk management strategy used to protect an investment against potential losses.
example: Investors use hedging to protect their portfolios against market volatility.
exampleVi: Các nhà đầu tư sử dụng biện pháp phòng ngừa rủi ro để bảo vệ danh mục đầu tư trước biến động thị trường.
collocations: hedging strategy, hedging contract, currency hedging

definition: The practice of buying an asset in one market and selling it in another at the same time to profit from price differences.
example: Traders used arbitrage to profit from price differences between the two markets.
exampleVi: Các nhà giao dịch đã sử dụng kinh doanh chênh lệch giá để kiếm lời từ sự khác biệt giá giữa hai thị trường.
collocations: arbitrage opportunity, regulatory arbitrage, arbitrage trading

definition: The activity of buying assets in the hope that their price will rise, involving high risk.
example: Speculation about the company's future drove up its stock price.
exampleVi: Sự đầu cơ về tương lai của công ty đã đẩy giá cổ phiếu tăng lên.
collocations: market speculation, currency speculation, trigger speculation

definition: The process of spreading investments across different areas or assets to reduce risk.
example: Diversification helps reduce the overall risk of an investment portfolio.
exampleVi: Đa dạng hóa giúp giảm thiểu rủi ro tổng thể của danh mục đầu tư.
collocations: portfolio diversification, product diversification, business diversification

vietnamese: sự tịch thu tài sản thế chấp
definition: The legal process by which a lender takes control of a property because the borrower failed to pay the mortgage.
example: The bank began foreclosure proceedings after he missed several mortgage payments.
exampleVi: Ngân hàng đã bắt đầu thủ tục tịch thu tài sản sau khi anh ấy không thanh toán khoản vay thế chấp nhiều lần.
collocations: foreclosure proceedings, face foreclosure, foreclosure rate

definition: The ease with which an asset can be converted into cash without affecting its market price.
example: High market liquidity makes it easy to buy and sell stocks quickly.
exampleVi: Tính thanh khoản thị trường cao giúp việc mua và bán cổ phiếu nhanh chóng dễ dàng hơn.
collocations: market liquidity, high liquidity, liquidity crisis, cash liquidity
